name: Gigalixir Elixir/Phoenix PaaS API
what_it_does: Elixir and Phoenix-focused Platform-as-a-Service with REST API — enabling Elixir developers to deploy Phoenix applications with full BEAM support, hot upgrades, clustering (distributed Erlang nodes), persistent connections (WebSockets, LiveView), managed PostgreSQL, and zero-downtime deploys, without managing the infrastructure complexity of running distributed BEAM applications.
use_cases: Deploying Phoenix LiveView applications via Gigalixir API for real-time web app hosting
Managing Gigalixir app replicas and scaling via API for automated capacity management
Configuring Gigalixir environment variables and secrets via API for deployment automation
Managing Gigalixir databases and free PostgreSQL via API for database provisioning
Querying Gigalixir app logs and metrics via API for Phoenix application monitoring
not_for: Non-Elixir/BEAM applications (Gigalixir is purpose-built for Elixir/Phoenix)
Ruby, Python, Node.js, or Go applications (use Heroku, Railway, or Render)
Teams without Elixir expertise (BEAM architecture knowledge required)
best_when: An Elixir/Phoenix team needs a managed PaaS that understands BEAM semantics — distributed clustering, hot upgrades, persistent connections, and Phoenix LiveView — without managing Kubernetes or Erlang/OTP infrastructure.
avoid_when: You're not building in Elixir/Erlang, need multi-language support, or require enterprise SLA.

auth.notes: API key from Gigalixir account. HTTP Basic auth with email + API key. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes — API key provides full account access. CLI (gigalixir) for local deployment. Git push deployment (git remote add gigalixir). No webhooks. Environment variables for secrets management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 1 app, 0.2 replicas (shared CPU), 1 free PostgreSQL DB (10K rows, 2 connections)
pricing.paid_tiers: Standard replica $0.096/replica/hour (~$69/month for 1 standard); Database from $20/month; Pay-as-you-go pricing
pricing.notes: Founded 2017 by Jesse Shieh. Bootstrapped and profitable. Purpose-built for Elixir and Phoenix. No credit card required for free tier. Pricing by replica size (0.2 to 8 standard replicas). Standard replica = 1 CPU + 1.2 GB RAM. Clustering support — Elixir nodes discover each other automatically. Hot code upgrades via Distillery/Mix releases. Used by real-time apps, IoT backends, and LiveView applications. Only major managed PaaS with native Elixir/BEAM support.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for app and database management. HTTP Basic auth (email + API key). Readable documentation. No webhooks — must poll for status. No OAuth2. US-only data residency. Free tier requires no credit card. Rate limits not documented. Elixir-specific platform — BEAM knowledge required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: App reads idempotent by unique name. Config reads idempotent by key. Replica scaling idempotent by count. App creation not idempotent — check by name first.
known_agent_gotchas: Elixir/Phoenix exclusive — not for other languages
HTTP Basic auth with email + API key — non-standard pattern for API clients
No webhooks — must poll for deployment and scaling status
Free tier 0.2 replica shares CPU — insufficient for production load
US-only data residency — not suitable for EU data residency requirements

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HTTP Basic auth with email + API key — use HTTPS only. Environment variables for secrets storage. No OAuth2 or scope granularity — single API key per account. No fine-grained access controls. Elixir BEAM VM provides process isolation. Database encryption at rest. No compliance certifications documented.
